forgotPassword.sent: If an account exists for that email, a reset link is on its way.

resetPassword.description: Choose a strong password you don't use elsewhere.
resetPassword.hint: At least 12 characters, with an uppercase letter, a lowercase letter and a number.
resetPassword.invalidLink: This reset link is invalid or incomplete. Request a new one.
resetPassword.success: Your password has been changed. You can now sign in with it.
resetPassword.genericError: Something went wrong. Check your connection and try again.

errors.oauth_not_configured: {provider} sign-in is not set up yet. Use email instead.
errors.oauth_no_email: We could not get a verified email from {provider}. Use email instead.
errors.oauth_invalid: That sign-in option is not available.

helpCentre.title: Help centre
helpCentre.description: Find answers, reach support, and learn how to get the most out of Adysre.

email.title: Email support
email.body: Reach our team directly — we usually reply within one business day.

docs.title: Documentation
docs.body: Guides to get started and make the most of every feature.

api.title: API reference
api.body: Endpoints, authentication and response formats for developers.

community.title: Browse the library
community.body: Explore components, icons, palettes and gradients you can use today.

helpCentre.emailBody: Email us and we'll get back to you as soon as we can.

faqs.a: The core library — components, icons, palettes and gradients — is free to browse, copy and use. Some premium content requires a paid plan.
Components ship with code for HTML, React, Next.js and more. Copy the format you need, or export a whole page from the playground.

faqs.q: Can I use the icons in commercial projects?
faqs.a: Yes. The icons are original and free to use in personal and commercial projects — copy them as SVG, JSX or React, or download PNGs.

faqs.q: How do I build a full page?
faqs.a: Open Components and choose ‘Customize your project’ to assemble a page from library sections, then export the complete code.

faqs.q: How do I change the language?
faqs.a: Use the language option in the profile menu at the top right. Your choice is remembered across visits.

faqs.q: How do I report a problem?
faqs.a: Email our support team with the details and a screenshot if you can — we investigate every report.

gettingStarted.title: Getting started
gettingStarted.description: A quick tour of Adysre and everything you can build with it.

steps.title: Explore the library
steps.body: Browse ready-made components, icons, colour palettes and gradients from the sidebar.

steps.title: Copy or customize
steps.body: Copy any snippet in your framework, or open an item to tweak it before you export.

steps.title: Assemble a page
steps.body: In Components, choose ‘Customize your project’ to build a full page from sections.

steps.title: Export the code
steps.body: Take the complete, production-ready code with you — copy it or download the files.

gettingStarted.apiCta: Building an integration? Read the API reference

apiReference.title: API reference
apiReference.description: The Adysre REST API — conventions, authentication and endpoints.
apiReference.baseUrlNote: All endpoints are served under a single, versioned base path. v1 is stable and will not break.
apiReference.authNote: Requests are authenticated with a short-lived access token issued at sign-in and sent as an HTTP-only cookie. Expired tokens are refreshed automatically.
apiReference.responseNote: Every response uses the same envelope. Successful responses carry a data payload (and meta for lists); errors carry a stable code and a human-readable message.

endpoints.login: Sign in and start a session

apiReference.endpointsNote: This is a representative subset. The full, always-current reference lands with the public API.

terms.title: Terms of service
terms.description: The terms that govern your use of Adysre.

sections.heading: Acceptance of terms
sections.body: By accessing or using Adysre, you agree to these terms. If you do not agree, please do not use the service.

sections.heading: Using the service
sections.body: You may use Adysre to browse, copy and adapt library content for your own projects. You are responsible for how you use the code and assets you take.

sections.heading: Accounts
sections.body: Some features require an account. Keep your credentials secure; you are responsible for activity under your account.

sections.heading: Acceptable use
sections.body: Do not misuse the service — no unlawful activity, no attempts to disrupt or reverse-engineer the platform, and no reselling the library as your own.

sections.heading: Intellectual property
sections.body: Library content may be used in your projects. The Adysre name, brand and platform remain our property.

sections.heading: Changes and termination
sections.body: We may update these terms or the service over time. Continued use after changes means you accept the updated terms.

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.description: How we handle your data at Adysre.

sections.heading: Information we collect
sections.body: We collect the information you provide (such as your name and email) and basic usage data needed to run and improve the service.

sections.heading: How we use information
sections.body: We use your information to provide the service, personalise your experience, respond to support requests, and keep the platform secure.

sections.heading: Cookies
sections.body: We use essential cookies for authentication and preferences such as language and theme. We do not sell your data.

sections.heading: Data sharing
sections.body: We do not sell personal data. We share it only with providers that help us run the service, under appropriate safeguards.

sections.heading: Your rights
sections.body: You may request access to, correction of, or deletion of your personal data. Contact us to exercise these rights.

sections.heading: Security
sections.body: We use industry-standard measures to protect your data. No system is perfectly secure, but we work to keep yours safe.

dmca.title: DMCA policy
dmca.description: How to report copyright concerns on Adysre.

sections.heading: Reporting infringement
sections.body: If you believe content on Adysre infringes your copyright, send us a notice with the details below and we will review it promptly.

sections.heading: What to include
sections.body: Identify the copyrighted work, the material you believe infringes it and where it appears, your contact details, and a good-faith statement.

sections.heading: Our response
sections.body: On receiving a valid notice, we may remove or disable access to the material and notify the party who posted it.

sections.heading: Counter-notice
sections.body: If you believe your content was removed in error, you may submit a counter-notice with your details and a statement made under penalty of perjury.

sections.heading: Repeat infringers
sections.body: We may suspend or terminate accounts that repeatedly infringe the rights of others.

sections.heading: Contact
sections.body: Send copyright notices to our support address. Please use ‘DMCA’ in the subject line so we can route it quickly.

cart.label: Cart
cart.emptyHint: Buy All Access Pass to get unlimited access to all products.

promo.title: Build faster with All-Access
promo.description: Get access to all templates, components and blocks for a one-time payment.

pricing.title: Get instant access to every prompt in the library
pricing.subtitle: One payment unlocks all 120+ prompts across 15 categories - plus every prompt we add, and the modules still to come.

features.allCategories: All 15 categories, from Portrait to Sci-Fi
features.fourModels: Tuned for GPT Image, FLUX, Midjourney and SDXL
features.yearUpdates: 1 year of new prompts and updates
features.lifetimeUpdates: Lifetime updates - every prompt we add
features.futureModules: Components and Templates when they ship
features.promptEditor: Customise any prompt before you copy it

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Pay once, create faster. If you don't find what you need here, reach us at {email}

lifetimeIncludes.a: Every one of the 120+ prompts across all 15 categories, plus every prompt added afterwards - at no extra cost. It also covers the Components and Templates modules once they ship. One payment, no renewal.

purchaseFlow.q: How does the purchase and access flow work?
purchaseFlow.a: Pick a plan, pay once, and your workspace is upgraded straight away. Premium prompts unlock across every device you sign in on - there is nothing to download or install.

freeVsPremium.q: What's the difference between free and premium prompts?
freeVsPremium.a: Free covers 41 prompts you can use immediately without a card. Premium unlocks the full library of 120+, including the more involved cinematic, editorial and fantasy setups, and the prompt editor for customising any of them.

models.q: Which AI models do the prompts work with?
models.a: Every prompt lists the models it is tuned for - GPT Image, FLUX, Midjourney and SDXL - and you can filter the library by model. Prompts keep their lens, lighting and colour-grade details, so they carry across generators.

refunds.a: We don't offer refunds because you get immediate, full access to every prompt in the library the moment you buy. Please review our refund policy for details.

help.q: Need help or have a question?
help.a: Email {email} and we will get back to you. Lifetime and Team plans get priority support with a faster turnaround.

checkout.recurring: Pay {price} then {price} every 12 months until canceled. You may cancel your subscription at any time.
checkout.oneTime: Pay {price} once. Lifetime access, no renewal.
checkout.disclaimer: The above email will be used to create your account. You will be redirected to our payment provider to complete your purchase.
checkout.unavailable: Checkout isn't available yet. Please email support to purchase.

components.title: Components
components.subtitle: Free, copy-paste components in HTML, CSS, Tailwind, React, Next.js and TypeScript. MIT licensed - use them anywhere.
components.searchPlaceholder: Search components by name, tag or category…

empty.title: No components match those filters
empty.description: Try a different search or clear the filters.

propDescriptions.afterSrc: The image shown on the "after" side.
propDescriptions.allowMultiple: Allows more than one item open at once.
propDescriptions.ariaLabel: The button's accessible name. Required for icon-only buttons.
propDescriptions.authorAvatarSrc: Image URL for the author's avatar.
propDescriptions.autoDismissAfter: How long before it dismisses itself, in seconds. Zero keeps it up.
propDescriptions.bandCopy: A short line of supporting copy shown in the call-to-action band.
propDescriptions.beforeSrc: The image shown on the "before" side.
propDescriptions.buildHref: Builds the URL for a page number, so pages stay real links.
propDescriptions.calloutLabel: The short heading above the callout, such as "Note".
propDescriptions.caption: The table's caption, announced to screen readers before its contents.
propDescriptions.category: Category label shown above the title.
propDescriptions.className: Additional classes merged onto the root element.
propDescriptions.columns: The columns being compared, in display order.
propDescriptions.comparisonPeriod: Comparison window shown beside the delta.
propDescriptions.copy: Body text shown under the title.
propDescriptions.count: Number shown in the badge; anything above 99 collapses to "99+".
propDescriptions.coverSrc: Image URL for the cover image.
propDescriptions.currentPage: The active page number, starting at 1.
propDescriptions.danger: Marks the item as destructive, so it reads as a warning.
propDescriptions.defaultAnnual: Whether annual billing is selected on first render.
propDescriptions.defaultCollapsed: Whether the sidebar starts collapsed to its icon rail.
propDescriptions.defaultFilter: The filter applied on first render.
propDescriptions.defaultOpenId: Id of the item expanded on mount.
propDescriptions.defaultOpenIds: Ids of the items expanded on mount.
propDescriptions.defaultOpenState: Whether it starts open on first render.
propDescriptions.defaultTabId: The tab selected on first render.
propDescriptions.delay: Seconds to wait before animating in.
propDescriptions.delta: Change since the comparison period, pre-formatted.
propDescriptions.direction: Direction the element travels from as it appears.
propDescriptions.disabled: Prevents interaction and dims the control.
propDescriptions.dismissLabel: Accessible name for the dismiss button.
propDescriptions.distance: Distance in pixels the element travels.
propDescriptions.excerpt: Short summary shown under the title.
propDescriptions.figureCaption: The caption describing the figure's contents.
propDescriptions.foundingLabel: A short line beneath the founding year that gives it context.
propDescriptions.groupName: Scopes the exclusive group. Pass a unique value per accordion on a page.
propDescriptions.groups: Feature groups, each with a heading and its own list.
propDescriptions.hasMore: Whether any items remain to load. When false the control retires.
propDescriptions.icon: Icon element rendered alongside the content.
propDescriptions.imageAlt: Alternative text for the image. Empty when the title already names it.
propDescriptions.imageSrc: Image URL for the card's picture.
propDescriptions.items: The array of items to render.
propDescriptions.kicker: Small label shown above the title.
propDescriptions.label: Accessible label announced while loading.
propDescriptions.lines: Number of placeholder lines to render.
propDescriptions.loading: Shows a spinner and blocks input while true.
propDescriptions.magnifyRange: How far the magnification reaches, in pixels either side of the pointer.
propDescriptions.maxScale: How large the icon under the pointer grows, as a multiplier.
propDescriptions.maxSeats: The most seats the slider allows.
propDescriptions.members: The people shown in the grid.
propDescriptions.meta: Short supporting facts shown beneath the heading.
propDescriptions.metrics: The metrics to display, each with a value and label.
propDescriptions.minSeats: The fewest seats the slider allows.
propDescriptions.name: Plan name shown as the heading.
propDescriptions.note: A small qualifying line, such as what the price excludes.
propDescriptions.onAddToCart: Fired when the user adds the product to the cart.
propDescriptions.once: Animates only the first time it enters view.
propDescriptions.onClick: Fired when the user activates the control.
propDescriptions.onClose: Called when the component asks to close - Escape, the backdrop, or the close control.
propDescriptions.onConfirm: Fired once the destructive action is confirmed.
propDescriptions.onDismiss: Fired when the user dismisses the notification.
propDescriptions.onLoadMore: Called when the next batch is requested.
propDescriptions.onPageSizeChange: Called with the new page size when it changes.
propDescriptions.onPeriodChange: Called with the billing period when it is toggled.
propDescriptions.onSelect: Fired with the menu item the user chose.
propDescriptions.onSignOut: Called when the sign-out item is chosen.
propDescriptions.onSubmit: Called with the form's values when it is submitted.
propDescriptions.open: Whether the component is currently shown.
propDescriptions.pageSize: How many items each page shows.
propDescriptions.paragraphs: The body copy, one string per paragraph.
propDescriptions.pathname: The current path. The item matching it is marked as the active page.
propDescriptions.period: Billing period shown beside the price.
propDescriptions.perSeatPrice: The price charged for each seat.
propDescriptions.personName: Name of the person shown as the heading.
propDescriptions.principles: The values, each with a name and explanation.
propDescriptions.progressValue: How far along the task is, from 0 to 100.
propDescriptions.quote: Testimonial text, without surrounding quotation marks.
propDescriptions.reviewCount: Number of reviews shown beside the rating.
propDescriptions.role: Job title shown beneath the name.
propDescriptions.rows: The alternating image-and-copy rows, in order.
propDescriptions.secondaryHref: Where the secondary action links to.
propDescriptions.services: The services to list, each with an icon, name and summary.
propDescriptions.severity: Tone of the notification - sets its colour and icon.
propDescriptions.siblingCount: How many page numbers to show either side of the current one.
propDescriptions.sliderPosition: Where the divider sits, as a percentage from the left.
propDescriptions.socials: Social profile links rendered as icons.
propDescriptions.statLabel: Name of the metric shown above the value.
propDescriptions.steps: The ordered steps of the process.
propDescriptions.tabItems: The tabs, each with a label and its panel content.
propDescriptions.text: String written to the clipboard when the button is pressed.
propDescriptions.tiers: The pricing tiers, in display order.
propDescriptions.title: Heading text for the card.
propDescriptions.totalItems: How many items exist across all pages.
propDescriptions.totalPages: How many pages exist in total.
propDescriptions.trend: Direction of the change - sets the delta's colour and arrow.

preview.title: Preview
preview.fullscreen: Open preview in a new tab

banner.title: Build your own page
banner.description: Assemble a page from library sections - header to footer - then copy or download the complete code.

empty.title: Nothing to preview

export.title: Your page code
export.description: Copy or download the code for your assembled page.
export.projectHint: A ready-to-run project. Unzip it, then run npm install.

welcome.title: Welcome to the Playground
welcome.body: Assemble a full page from ready-made sections - from header to footer - then take the code with you.

slots.title: Pick your sections
slots.body: Every section of your page lives here. Click one to work on it, or add and remove the optional ones.

variations.title: Try variations
variations.body: Each section ships in several designs. Click any variation to swap it into your page instantly.

canvas.title: Watch it come together
canvas.body: This is your page, assembled live. Hover a section to switch variations right on the preview.

device.title: Check every screen
device.body: Preview your page at desktop, tablet and phone widths.

export.title: Take the code
export.body: When it looks right, copy or download the complete code for every section.

upload.title: Start from a reference
upload.body: Drop in a sketch or a screenshot of a homepage you like and we'll assemble a matching template in seconds.

scratch.title: Or build from scratch
scratch.body: Prefer to choose everything yourself? Skip the upload and assemble the page section by section.

result.title: Your generated template
result.body: Here's your starting template. Download the code as-is, customize the sections, or start over with a new reference.

content.title: Customise the copy
content.body: Select any section, then edit its titles, text, labels and buttons here. Your changes show live and ship in the downloaded code.

start.title: Customize your project
start.subtitle: Upload a reference and we'll assemble a matching template - or start from a blank canvas.
start.scratchDescription: Pick every section and variation yourself.

upload.title: Drop a reference image here
upload.hint: A sketch, wireframe, or a screenshot of a homepage you like.
upload.formats: PNG, JPG, WEBP or GIF · up to 12 MB
upload.errorType: That file isn't a supported image. Use PNG, JPG, WEBP or GIF.
upload.errorSize: That image is over 12 MB. Try a smaller file.

generating.title: Building your template…
generating.subtitle: Analyzing your reference and assembling matching sections.
generating.error: We couldn't read that image. Please try another file.

result.title: Your template is ready
result.subtitle: {count} sections assembled from your reference. Download the code, or customize it further.

palette.label: Choose the project palette
palette.title: Project palette
palette.description: Choose the colour palette shipped with your generated code.

content.title: Edit content
content.empty: Add a section to edit its text, titles and labels.

fields.name: Website / brand name
fields.commentsPlaceholder: e.g. calm and premium, mostly light
fields.hexesHint: Enter hex codes or colour names, separated by commas.
fields.imageHint: We’ll pull the palette from its colours. PNG, JPG, WEBP or GIF.

generator.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.
generator.errorType: Unsupported image. Use PNG, JPG, WEBP or GIF.

welcome.title: Welcome to Color Palettes
welcome.body: Ready-made colour palettes for your website or app - copy, tweak, export, or generate your own.

generate.title: Generate your own
generate.body: Describe your brand, type in colours, or upload a reference image and we’ll build a palette for you.

browse.body: Click any colour to copy its hex, or copy the whole set. Search and filter by tag or colour.

edit.title: Edit and swap
edit.body: Open a palette to recolour any swatch, lock the ones you like, and shuffle the rest.

export.title: Export the code
export.body: Copy or download the palette as CSS, SCSS, Tailwind, an array or JSON - or save it as a PNG.

similar.title: Explore similar
similar.body: Jump to palettes with a matching mood to keep exploring.

icons.title: Icons
icons.subtitle: {count} original, open line icons for your product — search, recolour, resize, then copy or download as SVG, PNG, React or CSS.
icons.searchPlaceholder: Search icons by name, category or tag…

welcome.title: Welcome to Icons
welcome.body: A library of original, open line icons — browse, customise, and take the code in any format.

customize.title: Set the weight
customize.body: Drag to make every icon lighter or bolder. Your choice sticks across the whole library.

browse.title: Find an icon
browse.body: Search by name, category or tag, or filter by theme. Hover any icon to copy its SVG instantly.

edit.title: Make it yours
edit.body: Open an icon to recolour it, change its weight, resize it, or switch to a filled look — live.

export.title: Copy or export
export.body: Grab the icon as SVG, JSX, a React component, a data URI, or a CSS mask rule.

download.title: Download anywhere
download.body: Save a crisp SVG, or a PNG at any size from 16 up to 256 pixels.

meta.title: ADYSRE - Design and ship in one workspace
meta.description: The workspace where product teams browse components, icons, palettes, gradients and prompts, then take them straight to production.

hero.title: Everything your team needs to design and ship
hero.subtitle: ADYSRE brings components, icons, palettes, gradients and prompts together, so your team can move from idea to production without switching tools.

marquee.label: Built for the way modern product teams work

features.title: One workspace, every building block
features.subtitle: Each module is ready to browse, copy and drop into your product. Open any of them from the side menu once you are in.

components.title: Components
components.desc: Copy-paste UI blocks in HTML, Tailwind, React and Next.js, typed and ready for production.

icons.title: Icons
icons.desc: A tuned icon set with adjustable weight, live preview and one-click copy in your format.

palettes.title: Color palettes
palettes.desc: Accessible palettes with token output, so brand colours stay consistent everywhere.

gradients.title: Gradients
gradients.desc: Layered, on-brand gradients with CSS you can paste straight into your styles.

patterns.title: Patterns
patterns.desc: Repeatable CSS background patterns you can paste straight into your styles.

textures.title: Textures
textures.desc: Noise, grain, paper and woven surface textures in pure CSS - no image files.

promptLibrary.title: Prompt library
promptLibrary.desc: A curated library of prompts across categories, tuned for the models your team already uses.

templates.title: Templates
templates.desc: Full page and flow templates that assemble the modules into finished screens.

workflow.title: From blank canvas to shipped, in three steps
workflow.subtitle: The same loop for every module, so the whole team moves the same way.

browse.title: Browse
browse.desc: Search across every module and preview each result live before you commit.

tailor.title: Tailor
tailor.desc: Adjust colour, weight and framework, then copy output that matches your tokens.

ship.title: Ship
ship.desc: Paste it into your product and keep moving, with no cleanup and no lock-in.

showcase.title: See it work before you commit
showcase.subtitle: Live previews built from the same design tokens your product uses.

templates.title: Full templates are on the way
templates.subtitle: Complete pages and flows, assembled from the modules you already use and ready to make your own. Get told the moment they land.
templates.note: Included with every plan, at no extra cost.

faq.title: Questions, answered
faq.subtitle: Everything else you might want to know before you start.

what.a: A single workspace for design and build resources: components, icons, palettes, gradients and prompts, ready to copy into your product.

frameworks.a: Components ship as HTML, CSS, Tailwind, React, Next.js and TypeScript, so you can drop them into most modern stacks.

templates.a: Templates are in active development. Open the templates page to preview what is coming and be first to know when it ships.

pricing.a: Yes. You can browse and use a generous free selection with no card. One payment unlocks everything, including what we add later.

team.q: Can my whole team use it?
team.a: Yes. Team access shares one workspace across your organisation, with the same modules for everyone.

cta.title: Ready to build with less friction?
cta.subtitle: Open the workspace and browse every module in the side menu. No setup, no cleanup.

footer.tagline: The workspace where teams design and ship together.

announcement.label: Icons, Gradients and Palettes are now live in the workspace
